title: बद्रीनाथ आरती
devanagariText:
॥ श्री बद्रीनाथ आरती ॥

जय बदरी विशाल जी, तुम्हारी जय हो।
चार धाम में भू धाम, तुम्हारी जय हो॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

अगणित कल्पों से, सेवत नर नारी।
गंगा बहत यहाँ पर, सब पापन हारी॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

शंख चक्र गदा पद्म, चारि भुज धारी।
वनमाला विराजत, कौस्तुभ मणि भारी॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

नर नारायण के तुम, अवतारी देवा।
ब्रह्मा शंकर आदिक, करत तव सेवा॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

बद्री का बन पावन, तप करने आये।
उद्धव ने शिक्षा ली, गोपी उपदेश बताये॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

माता लक्ष्मी संग प्रभु, बद्री में राजे।
अलकनन्दा तट पर, मंदिर के साजे॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

शीतकाल में बन्द होत, गरमी में खुलता।
षट्‌मास देवता पूजत, षट्‌मास न मिलता॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

तप्तकुण्ड का जल गरम, शीतल वन सोहे।
बद्रीनाथ के दर्शन, सब जन मन मोहे॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

श्री बदरीनाथ जी की, आरती जो गावे।
कहत सदा भक्तन को, मोक्ष पद पावे॥

जय बदरी विशाल॥

॥ इति श्री बद्रीनाथ आरती सम्पूर्ण ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Badrinath Aarti ||

Jai Badri Vishal Ji, Tumhari Jai Ho.
Char Dham Mein Bhu Dham, Tumhari Jai Ho.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Aganit Kalpon Se, Sevat Nar Nari.
Ganga Bahat Yahan Par, Sab Papan Hari.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Shankh Chakra Gada Padma, Chari Bhuj Dhari.
Vanmala Virajat, Kaustubh Mani Bhari.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Nar Narayan Ke Tum, Avatari Deva.
Brahma Shankar Aadik, Karat Tav Seva.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Badri Ka Ban Pavan, Tap Karne Aaye.
Uddhav Ne Shiksha Li, Gopi Updesh Bataye.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Mata Lakshmi Sang Prabhu, Badri Mein Raje.
Alaknanda Tat Par, Mandir Ke Saje.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Shitkal Mein Band Hot, Garmi Mein Khulta.
Shatmas Devta Pujat, Shatmas Na Milta.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Taptkund Ka Jal Garam, Sheetal Van Sohe.
Badrinath Ke Darshan, Sab Jan Man Mohe.

Jai Badri Vishal.

Shri Badrinath Ji Ki, Aarti Jo Gave.
Kahat Sada Bhaktan Ko, Moksh Pad Pave.

Jai Badri Vishal.

|| Iti Shri Badrinath Aarti Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
जय बद्री विशाल! चार धाम में से एक भू धाम आपकी जय हो। अगणित कल्पों से नर-नारी सेवा करते हैं। यहाँ गंगा (अलकनन्दा) बहती है जो सब पापों को हर लेती है।

शंख, चक्र, गदा, पद्म चार भुजाओं में धारण किये, वनमाला और कौस्तुभ मणि विराजमान। नर-नारायण के अवतार, ब्रह्मा-शंकर आदि सेवा करते हैं।
benefits: Invokes the blessing of the merit of badrinath dham pilgrimage - one of the char dhams
Invokes moksha and liberation from the cycle of birth and death
Invokes Lord Vishnu as Badri Vishal for divine protection
Purifies all sins through the grace of Badrinath
Performed during the sacred opening season of Badrinath temple
description: Dedicated to Lord Vishnu, the Badrinath Aarti (बद्रीनाथ आरती) is a traditional worship song (aarti) revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Traditional Aarti Sangrah - Badrinath Temple, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant moksha and liberation from the cycle of birth and death as well as performed during the sacred opening season of Badrinath temple. Additionally, it is known to purify all sins through the grace of Badrinath. It is especially recommended on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Badrinath Temple Opening and Dev Deepawali for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 5 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Badrinath Aarti in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Badrinath Aarti in Hindi with meaning and transliteration. Jai Badri Vishal - sacred aarti of Char Dham Badrinath temple.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Badrinath Aarti?
seoFaqs.answer: The Badrinath Aarti, beginning with the famous line 'Pawan Mand Sugandh Sheetal, Hem Mandir Shobhitam,' is the traditional aarti sung in honour of Lord Badri Vishal, an incarnation of Vishnu enshrined at the Badrinath temple in Uttarakhand. Badrinath is one of the four Char Dhams of Vishnu and one of the 108 Divya Desams of the Sri Vaishnava tradition. The aarti praises the deity, the golden temple, the cool fragrant breeze, and the sacred Alakananda Ganga that flows nearby. (Source: ServDharm; Pujahome.)

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Pawan Mand Sugandh Sheetal' describe?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening line translates as 'gentle, fragrant, cool breeze,' evoking the pristine high-altitude environment of Badrinath at 3,300 metres in the Garhwal Himalayas, with the Neelkanth peak in the background and the icy Alakananda flowing past the temple. 'Hem Mandir Shobhitam' refers to the golden-spired temple, and 'Nikat Ganga Bahat Nirmal' to the pure Ganga (Alakananda) flowing nearby. The aarti is essentially a sensory portrait of the dham itself, intended to transport the devotee mentally to Badrinath. (Source: Chalisa PDF; Dharmsaar.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Badrinath Aarti sung?
seoFaqs.answer: Inside the Badrinath temple, the aarti is sung as part of the daily Maha Abhishek and Shayan Aarti rituals during the six-month yatra season (typically Akshaya Tritiya in late April or early May to Bhai Dooj in late October or early November), when the temple is open to pilgrims. Devotees who cannot reach Badrinath sing the aarti at home, particularly on Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Vasant Panchami (when the next year's opening date is announced), Akshaya Tritiya, and on the temple closing day. (Source: VividhGyan; The Divine India.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Badri Vishal worshipped at Badrinath?
seoFaqs.answer: Badri Vishal is the form of Vishnu enshrined at Badrinath, depicted in a meditative posture (padmasana) with Nara-Narayana iconography. The Skanda Purana and the Bhagavata Purana relate that Vishnu performed tapas at this site for the welfare of humanity, sheltered by Goddess Lakshmi who took the form of a Badri (jujube) tree to protect him from the snow. The shrine is one of the four Char Dhams (alongside Dwarka, Puri, and Rameshwaram) and a central pilgrimage of Sanatana Dharma. (Source: ServDharm; Pujahome.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Badrinath Aarti different from other Vishnu aartis?
seoFaqs.answer: Most popular Vishnu aartis, such as 'Om Jai Jagdish Hare,' praise the supreme Vishnu in his universal aspect. The Badrinath Aarti is sthala-specific, meaning it is rooted in the geography, iconography, and tradition of one particular sacred site. It evokes the Himalayan setting, the Alakananda, the golden spire, and the seated meditative form of Badri Narayan. It is therefore part of the larger genre of dham-specific aartis, alongside Kedarnath, Jagannath Puri, and Tirupati Balaji aartis. (Source: VividhGyan; Pujahome.)
